what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG line icon depicting people, with one full figure and a partial secondary figure, using currentColor for stroke color.
observations: Root svg has xmlns, viewBox, width, height, fill, stroke, stroke-width, stroke-linecap, and stroke-linejoin attributes.
The icon uses no fill and renders through stroked paths.
Stroke color is inherited via currentColor.
Two path elements define the visible geometry.
The first path draws a full head-and-shoulders figure and a lower arc for the body.
The second path draws a partial secondary head/body figure to the right.
standouts: Monochrome currentColor styling makes the icon context-colored.
The composition communicates a group or people concept with minimal path geometry.
conventions: Uses a 24x24 icon viewBox and explicit 24px width and height.
Uses round stroke caps and joins for a soft line-icon style.
Keeps all geometry inline in path d attributes.
concerns: No title, role, aria-label, or other accessibility metadata is present.
Dense inline path data makes visual edits less self-describing.
notes: A compact reusable SVG people/group icon drawn as a rounded stroked line asset.
questions: Should this SVG be decorative only, or should it expose an accessible label when used?
Is the 1.5 stroke width the intended standard for this icon set?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename and content align: it is a people/group icon.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a scalable interface icon for people, users, teams, or group-related navigation/actions.
contribution: A reusable monochrome people/group symbol that can inherit interface color and fit a 24px icon system.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 currentColor SVG line icon showing a primary person with a partial secondary person to represent people or a group.
intention_for_embedding: It was for representing people, users, teams, or group-related concepts in an interface icon set.
